visible_target: A single authored Sherman-family tank whose hull, turret, wheels, track wells, barrel, and coaxial MG read as coherent joined metal, with no visible no-op from prior rejected builds.
current_actual_read: User reports v1-15 is no-op churn: source and tokens changed, but the visible tank did not materially improve.
forbidden_old_mistake: Do not add small panels, plugs, blockers, pasted turret plates, source-token proofs, stale diagnostic renders, or browser wake as a substitute for visible relationship change.
single_edit_class: Evidence-gated redesign or one named exterior surface repair only; no broad tank improvement pass and no multiple speculative geometry tweaks in one commit.
accepted_evidence_needed: Cloud-hosted Sense Simulation verdict for the exact build token, naming expected relationship, actual relationship, what visibly changed, what did not change, and whether diagnostics agree or contradict the visual read.
what_would_prove_no_op: A fresh cloud/Sense or user visual report says the visible silhouette, turret/wheel/gap relationship, or armor continuity is materially unchanged despite source, GLB, or token changes.
next_allowed_actions: Refresh diagnostic renders only to understand failure, not to claim success.
Choose repair versus replacement from visual evidence before editing geometry.
If repairing, alter the exterior armor surface itself rather than adding blockers or decorative panels.
If replacing, build fewer larger coherent metal volumes with Sherman-readable silhouette.
dominant_shape_rule: Future geometry work must replace or materially reshape a dominant exterior form: hull/sponson shell, track slab/skirt volume, turret mass, or full track-well armor surface. Detail-only mesh changes are forbidden.
what_not_to_build: wheel-only readability tweaks while track slabs dominate
